children.heading: Foreign country of concern
children.label: 1 Foreign country of concern
children.text: The term “foreign country of concern” means the People’s Republic of China, the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea, the Russian Federation, the Islamic Republic of Iran, or any other country determined to be a country of concern by the Department of State.

children.label: A
children.text: designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the Secretary of State under ;section 1189(a) of title 8

children.label: B
children.text: included on the list of specially designated nationals and blocked persons maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Department of the Treasury (commonly known as the SDN list);

children.label: C
children.text: owned by, controlled by, or subject to the jurisdiction or direction of a government of a foreign country that is a covered nation (as such term is defined in );section 4872 of title 10

children.label: i
children.text: chapter 37 of title 18 (commonly known as the Espionage Act);

children.label: ii
children.text: section 951 or 1030 of title 18;

children.label: iii
children.text: chapter 90 of title 18 (commonly known as the Economic Espionage Act of 1996);

children.label: iv
children.text: the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: v
children.text: section 2274, 2275, 2276, 2277, or 2284 of this title;

children.label: vi
children.text: the Export Control Reform Act of 2018 ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 4801

children.label: vii
children.text: the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: E
children.text: determined by the Secretary of Commerce, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and the Director of National Intelligence, to be engaged in unauthorized conduct that is detrimental to the national security or foreign policy of the United States.

children.heading: National security strategy
children.label: 3 National security strategy
children.text: The term “national security strategy” means the national security strategy required under .section 3043 of title 50

children.label: A
children.text: review such strategy, including the national defense strategy under subsection (g) of and the national science and technology strategy under , programs, and resources as the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy determines pertain to United States’ national competitiveness in science, technology, research, innovation, and technology transfer activities, including patenting and licensing, that support the national security strategy;section 113 of title 10section 6615 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: develop or revise a national strategy to improve the national competitiveness of United States science, technology, research, and innovation to support the national security strategy; and

children.label: i
children.text: a report on the findings of the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy with respect to the review conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the strategy developed or revised pursuant to subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 2 Termination
children.text: This subsection terminates on the date that is 5 years after .August 9, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: An assessment of the efforts of the United States Government to preserve United States leadership in key emerging technologies and prevent United States strategic competitors from leveraging advanced technologies to gain strategic military or economic advantages over the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: An assessment of public and private investment in science and technology relevant to national security purposes, and the implications of such for the geostrategic position of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the prioritized economic security interests and objectives.

children.label: D
children.text: An assessment of global trends in science and technology, including potential threats to the national security of the United States in science and technology.

children.label: E
children.text: An assessment of the national debt and its implications for the economic and national security of the United States.

children.label: F
children.text: An assessment of how regional innovation capacity efforts in STEM fields are contributing and could contribute to the national security the United States, including programs run by State and local governments.

children.label: i
children.text: Workforce needs for competitiveness in technology areas identified in the national security strategy.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any efforts needed to expand pathways into technology fields to achieve the goals of the national security strategy.

children.label: H
children.text: An assessment of barriers to the development, evolution, or competitiveness of start-ups, small and mid-sized business entities, and industries that are critical to national security.

children.label: I
children.text: An assessment of the effectiveness of the Federal Government, federally funded research and development centers, and national laboratories in supporting and promoting the technology commercialization and technology transfer of technologies critical to national security.

children.label: J
children.text: An assessment of manufacturing capacity, logistics, and supply chain dynamics of major export sectors that are critical to national security, including access to a skilled workforce, physical infrastructure, and broadband network infrastructure.

children.label: K
children.text: An assessment of how the Federal Government is increasing the participation of underrepresented populations in science, research, innovation, and manufacturing.

children.label: i
children.text: the structure of current defense technology research and commercialization arrangements with regard to public-private partnerships; and

children.label: I
children.text: is being used to manufacture in the United States rather than in other countries; and

children.label: aa
children.text: any governmental organization of a foreign country of concern; or

children.label: AA
children.text: known to be owned or controlled by any governmental organization of a foreign country of concern; or

children.label: BB
children.text: organized under, or otherwise subject to, the laws of a foreign country of concern.

children.label: M
children.text: Recommendations to enhance the ability of the Federal Government to recruit into Federal service and retain in such service individuals with critical skills relevant to national security.

children.label: N
children.text: Recommendations for policies to protect United States leadership and the allies of the United States in critical areas relevant to national security through targeted export controls, investment screening, and counterintelligence activities.

children.label: O
children.text: Informed by the interagency process established under section 1758 of the Export Control Reform Act of 2018 [], a technology annex, which may be classified, describing an integrated and enduring approach to the identification, prioritization, development, and fielding of emerging technologies relevant to national security.50 U.S.C. 4817

children.label: i
children.text: Specific objectives, tasks, metrics, and milestones for each relevant Federal agency.

children.label: ii
children.text: Strategic objectives and priorities necessary to maintain the leadership of the United States in science and technology relevant to national security purposes, including near-term, medium-term, and long-term research priorities.

children.label: iii
children.text: Specific plans to safeguard research and technology funded, as appropriate, in whole or in part, by the Federal Government, including in technologies critical to national security, from theft or exfiltration by foreign entities of concern.

children.label: iv
children.text: Specific plans to support public and private sector investment in research, technology development, education and workforce development, and domestic manufacturing supportive of the national security of the United States and to foster the use of public-private partnerships.

children.label: I
children.text: How the strategy submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(ii) supports the national security strategy.

children.label: aa
children.text: national defense strategy under subsection (g) of ; andsection 113 of title 10

children.label: bb
children.text: national science and technology strategy under .section 6615 of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: A plan to encourage the governments of countries that are allies or partners of the United States to cooperate with the execution of such strategy, where appropriate.

children.label: vii
children.text: A plan for strengthening the industrial base of the United States.

children.label: viii
children.text: A plan to remove or update overly burdensome or outdated Federal regulations, as appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: to further incentivize industry participation in public-private partnerships for the purposes of accelerating technology research and commercialization in support of national security, including alternate ways of accounting for in-kind contributions and valuing partially manufactured products;

children.label: II
children.text: to ensure that intellectual property developed with Federal funding is commercialized in the United States; and

children.label: aa
children.text: any governmental organization of a foreign country of concern; or

children.label: AA
children.text: known to be owned or controlled by any governmental organization of a foreign country of concern; or

children.label: BB
children.text: organized under, or otherwise subject to, the laws of a foreign country of concern.

children.label: x
children.text: An identification of additional resources, administrative action, or legislative action recommended to assist with the implementation of such strategy.

children.heading: Research and development funding
children.label: d Research and development funding
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy shall, as the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy considers necessary, consult with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget and with the heads of such other elements of the Executive Office of the President as the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy considers appropriate to ensure the recommendations and priorities with respect to research and development funding relevant to national security, as expressed in the most recent report and strategy submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C) are incorporated into the development of annual budget requests for Federal research agencies.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: e Publication
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy shall, consistent with the protection of national security and other sensitive matters and to the maximum extent practicable, make each report submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i) publicly available on an internet website of the Office of Science and Technology Policy. Each such report may include a classified annex if the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy determines such is appropriate.
